pr_summary: This PR adds bulk fetch and fixes password reset functionality to the userController, but introduces several critical security and performance issues
verdict_reason: The PR is rejected due to critical security vulnerabilities, such as storing passwords in plaintext and missing authorization checks, as well as performance issues like N+1 queries and sequential awaits

findings.line_content: await db.query('UPDATE users SET password = ? WHERE email = ?',
findings.title: Plaintext Password
findings.description: The password is being stored in plaintext, which is a significant security risk. If an attacker gains access to the database, they will have access to all user passwords.
findings.suggestion: Use a password hashing library like bcrypt to hash the password before storing it. For example: `const hashedPassword = await bcrypt.hash(newPassword, 10); await db.query('UPDATE users SET password = ? WHERE email = ?', [hashedPassword, email]);

findings.title: Missing Authorization
findings.description: The code is fetching users by id without verifying if the requesting user has permission to access those users.
findings.suggestion: Add authorization checks to ensure the requesting user has permission to access the users. For example: `if (!req.user.isAdmin) { return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Forbidden' }); }`

findings.title: Missing null check on req.query
findings.description: req.query can be null or undefined, causing a crash when trying to access its properties.
findings.suggestion: Add a null check before accessing req.query: `if (req.query) { ... }`

findings.title: Missing null check on ids
findings.description: ids can be null or undefined, causing a crash when trying to call split() on it.
findings.suggestion: Add a null check before calling split() on ids: `if (ids) { const userIds = ids.split(','); }`

findings.title: N+1 query
findings.description: This loop issues a database query for each user ID, leading to a large number of queries and potential performance issues. This can be optimized by using a single query with an IN clause.
findings.suggestion: Use a single query with an IN clause: `const users = await db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id IN (?)', [userIds]);`

findings.title: Missing Authorization
findings.description: The code is fetching activity logs for a user without verifying if the requesting user has permission to access those logs.
findings.suggestion: Add authorization checks to ensure the requesting user has permission to access the activity logs. For example: `if (req.user.id !== userId) { return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Forbidden' }); }`

findings.title: Sequential awaits
findings.description: The `map` function is executed sequentially, leading to potential performance issues. This can be optimized by using `Promise.all` to execute the queries concurrently.
findings.suggestion: Use `Promise.all` to execute the queries concurrently: `const enriched = await Promise.all(logs.map(log => { ... }));`

findings.title: Missing null check on users
findings.description: users can be null or undefined, causing a crash when trying to access its properties.
findings.suggestion: Add a null check before accessing users: `if (users) { return { ...log, user: users[log.user_id] }; }`

findings.title: Empty input handling
findings.description: If the 'ids' query parameter is empty, this will throw an error. It should be handled to prevent crashes.

findings.line_content: const user = await db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ?', [id]);
findings.title: Missing error handling for db.query
findings.description: db.query can reject with an error, causing the function to crash if not handled.
findings.suggestion: Add try-catch block to handle errors: `try { const user = await db.query(...); } catch (error) { ... }`

findings.line_content: const user = await db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE email = ?', [email]);
findings.title: Missing error handling for db.query
findings.description: db.query can reject with an error, causing the function to crash if not handled.
findings.suggestion: Add try-catch block to handle errors: `try { const user = await db.query(...); } catch (error) { ... }`

findings.title: Edge case: missing user record
findings.description: If the user with the given ID does not exist, this will throw an error. It should be handled to prevent crashes.

positive_observations: The PR attempts to add useful functionality like bulk fetch and password reset
The code is generally well-structured and easy to follow
missing_tests: Test cases for authorization checks on user fetch and activity logs
Test cases for password hashing and verification
